q: Silence is a source of great strength.
h:
“Silence is a source of great strength.” —
Lao Tzu

q: Change is never easy, but always possible.
h:
“Change is never easy, but always possible.” —
Barack Obama

q: Be approximately right rather than exactly wrong.
h:
“Be approximately right rather than exactly wrong.” —
John Tukey

q: Be grateful for what you have now, and nothing should be taken for granted.
h:
“Be grateful for what you have now, and nothing should be taken for granted.” —
Roy T. Bennett

q: So we beat on, boats against the current, borne back ceaselessly into the past.
h:
“So we beat on, boats against the current, borne back ceaselessly into the past.” —
F. Scott Fitzgerald

q: The trouble with the rat race is that even if you win, you're still a rat.
h:
“The trouble with the rat race is that even if you win, you're still a rat.” —
Lily Tomlin

q: When you are able to employ your will always for constructive purposes, you become the controller of your destiny.
h:
“When you are able to employ your will always for constructive purposes, you become the controller of your destiny.” —
Paramahansa Yogananda

q: Freedom is the only worthy goal in life. It is won by disregarding things that lie beyond our control.
h:
“Freedom is the only worthy goal in life. It is won by disregarding things that lie beyond our control.” —
Epictetus

q: If you want to see things just as they are, then you yourself must practice just as you are.
h:
“If you want to see things just as they are, then you yourself must practice just as you are.” —
Dogen

q: Dreams have only one owner at a time. That's why dreamers are lonely.
h:
“Dreams have only one owner at a time. That's why dreamers are lonely.” —
William Faulkner

q: There are no accidents... there is only some purpose that we haven't yet understood.
h:
“There are no accidents... there is only some purpose that we haven't yet understood.” —
Deepak Chopra

q: The heart wants what it wants. There's no logic.
h:
“The heart wants what it wants. There's no logic. ” —
Woody Allen

q: Being wrong brings the opportunity for growth.
h:
“Being wrong brings the opportunity for growth.” —
Mark Manson

q: If we know how to adapt, we end up being superior.
h:
“If we know how to adapt, we end up being superior.” —
Ming-Dao Deng

q: Man suffers only because he takes seriously what the gods made for fun.
h:
“Man suffers only because he takes seriously what the gods made for fun.” —
Alan Watts

q: Don't ever save anything for a special occasion. Being alive is the special occasion.
h:
“Don't ever save anything for a special occasion. Being alive is the special occasion.” —
Mary Engelbreit

q: Respect other people's feelings. It might mean nothing to you, but it could mean everything to them.
h:
“Respect other people's feelings. It might mean nothing to you, but it could mean everything to them.” —
Roy T. Bennett

q: Opportunity often comes disguised in the form of misfortune or temporary defeat.
h:
“Opportunity often comes disguised in the form of misfortune or temporary defeat.” —
Napoleon Hill

q: We have no more right to consume happiness without producing it than to consume wealth without producing it.
h:
“We have no more right to consume happiness without producing it than to consume wealth without producing it.” —
George Bernard Shaw

q: To be angry is to revenge the faults of others on ourselves.
h:
“To be angry is to revenge the faults of others on ourselves.” —
Alexander Pope

q: All religions, arts and sciences are branches of the same tree.
h:
“All religions, arts and sciences are branches of the same tree. ” —
Albert Einstein

q: Knowing is not enough, we must apply. Willing is not enough, we must do.
h:
“Knowing is not enough, we must apply. Willing is not enough, we must do. ” —
Bruce Lee

q: Find the game where you can win, and then commit your life to playing it; and play to win.
h:
“Find the game where you can win, and then commit your life to playing it; and play to win.” —
Robert Kiyosaki

q: Better late than never.
h:
“Better late than never.” —
Spencer Johnson

q: Comedy = tragedy + time.
h:
“Comedy = tragedy + time.” —
Carol Burnett

q: Leadership starts at the top.
h:
“Leadership starts at the top.” —
Morgan Wootten

q: Throw your heart over the fence and the rest will follow
h:
“Throw your heart over the fence and the rest will follow” —
Norman Vincent Peale

q: Everything eaten is killed. Every meal is a sacrifice.
h:
“Everything eaten is killed. Every meal is a sacrifice.” —
Adi Da Samraj

q: Action may not always bring happiness, but there is no happiness without action.
h:
“Action may not always bring happiness, but there is no happiness without action.” —
William James

q: Successful people are not without problems. They're simply people who've learned to solve their problems.
h:
“Successful people are not without problems. They're simply people who've learned to solve their problems.” —
Earl Nightingale

q: A simple rule in dealing with those who are hard to get along with is to remember that this person is striving to assert his superiority; and you must deal with him from that point of view.
h:
“A simple rule in dealing with those who are hard to get along with is to remember that this person is striving to assert his superiority; and you must deal with him from that point of view.” —
Alfred Adler

q: We are most nearly ourselves when we achieve the seriousness of the child at play.
h:
“We are most nearly ourselves when we achieve the seriousness of the child at play.” —
Heraclitus

q: You have a brain and mind of your own. Use it, and reach your own decisions.
h:
“You have a brain and mind of your own. Use it, and reach your own decisions.” —
Napoleon Hill

q: Identification with our mind causes thought to become compulsive.
h:
“Identification with our mind causes thought to become compulsive.” —
Eckhart Tolle

q: The giving of love is an education in itself.
h:
“The giving of love is an education in itself.” —
Eleanor Roosevelt

q: Force has no place where there is need of skill.
h:
“Force has no place where there is need of skill. ” —
Herodotus

q: If you do not get the chills when you set your goal you're not setting big enough goals.
h:
“If you do not get the chills when you set your goal you're not setting big enough goals.” —
Bob Proctor

q: Your home is where your thoughts find peace.
h:
“Your home is where your thoughts find peace.” —
Zen Proverb

q: A good system shortens the road to the goal.
h:
“A good system shortens the road to the goal. ” —
Orison Swett Marden

q: If you're not failing every now and again, it's a sign you're not doing anything very innovative.
h:
“If you're not failing every now and again, it's a sign you're not doing anything very innovative.” —
Woody Allen

q: Do good, live in the most positive and joyful way possible every day.
h:
“Do good, live in the most positive and joyful way possible every day.” —
Roy T. Bennett

q: If anything is worth doing, do it with all your heart.
h:
“If anything is worth doing, do it with all your heart.” —
Buddha

q: The positive thinker sees the invisible, feels the intangible, and achieves the impossible.
h:
“The positive thinker sees the invisible, feels the intangible, and achieves the impossible.” —
Winston Churchill

q: In order to control myself I must first accept myself by going with and not against my nature.
h:
“In order to control myself I must first accept myself by going with and not against my nature.” —
Bruce Lee

q: To travel is to be alive, but to get somewhere is to be dead.
h:
“To travel is to be alive, but to get somewhere is to be dead.” —
Alan Watts

q: Sad thing is, you can still love someone and be wrong for them.
h:
“Sad thing is, you can still love someone and be wrong for them.” —
Elvis Presley

q: Your time is limited, so don't waste it living someone elses. life. Don't be trapped by dogma - which is living with the results of other people's thinking.
h:
“Your time is limited, so don't waste it living someone elses. life. Don't be trapped by dogma - which is living with the results of other people's thinking.” —
Steve Jobs

q: Goals are the fuel in the furnace of achievement.
h:
“Goals are the fuel in the furnace of achievement.” —
Brian Tracy

q: No one ever is defeated until defeat has been accepted as a reality.
h:
“No one ever is defeated until defeat has been accepted as a reality.” —
Napoleon Hill

q: No valid plans for the future can be made by those who have no capacity for living now.
h:
“No valid plans for the future can be made by those who have no capacity for living now.” —
Alan Watts
